title: Drive the single-device execute path with bounded real evidence

stories.title: Make network/log ensure outcomes honest through template/apply/save stages
stories.scope: Refine the single-device onboarding ensure path so network/log onboarding no longer stops at a success-leaning dispatch summary. When syslog template selection, remote apply, or save cannot be completed through a concrete contract, the stored onboarding actions must preserve blocked, failed, or unknown outcomes with exact reasons instead of collapsing back to optimistic success wording.
nonGoals: Do not implement full SNMP trap delivery.
Do not guess remote success when template/apply/save evidence is missing.
acceptance: Single-device onboarding evidence keeps non-success detail through network/log ensure stages instead of reverting to generic success wording.
Template-required, apply-failed, save-failed, and similar missing-contract states remain explicit in onboarding action evidence.
Backend tests cover at least one network/log non-success path beyond the already-fixed synthetic syslog success default.
notes: This is the owner-directed continuation of the honest execute-path mainline, not a closure-only validation batch.
Keep the contract single-device only; batch execution remains out of scope.

stories.title: Run a bounded single-device continue-onboarding probe and capture durable evidence
stories.scope: Use the authorized single-device continue-onboarding path to run one bounded execute probe, then record durable evidence of the actual monitor/log action outcomes or the exact concrete blocker. The evidence must distinguish local contract/test validation from any real side-effectful onboarding execution and must not hide partial or blocked results behind a generic success summary.
nonGoals: Do not perform batch onboarding execution.
Do not claim real remote success without durable evidence.
Do not reopen browser-only validation as the main task.
acceptance: A durable evidence summary is written for one bounded single-device execute probe.
The evidence clearly states whether the probe reached real monitor/log action execution or stopped at a concrete blocker.
Any non-success, retryable, blocked, or unknown outcome from the probe is preserved explicitly in the evidence.
notes: If the bounded probe exposes a concrete runtime blocker, record it honestly and stop there; do not fabricate success to keep the loop moving.

stories.title: Carry the bounded execute-probe result into durable readiness evidence
stories.scope: After the bounded execute probe, update the durable readiness and review evidence so the next loop handoff is driven by real single-device execute results rather than stale browser-only conclusions. If the probe uncovers one final frontend/backend honesty mismatch within the allowed paths, fix it before writing the evidence summary; otherwise document the exact remaining blocker and next continuation rule.
nonGoals: Do not close the overall program unless the execute-path evidence truly supports closure.
Do not create another browser-only micro round.
acceptance: final-readiness.md and review.md reflect the bounded execute-probe result rather than the old browser-only gap framing.
If a concrete frontend/backend honesty mismatch remains inside allowed paths, it is fixed and validated before the evidence summary is finalized.
The next-step note clearly says whether the loop should continue with another execution batch or whether closure is finally justified.
notes: This story is the execution-side close-out for batch-005; it should leave the next loop handoff driven by real execute-path evidence.
